sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see 9 green blocks:
- Top row: 3 blocks at rows 13-17, cols 25-29, 31-35, 37-41
- Middle rows: 6 blocks in 2x3 grid at rows 31-35 and 37-41, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29
The agent is at row 37-40, col 50-51 (right edge of blue region).
The blue region now has 2 separate objects (1757 pixels total), compared to 1 object (2056 pixels) before. This means the blue region split again.
The black regions have 2 objects (1898 pixels), same as before.
The gray dots increased from 150 to 180.
The white bar decreased from 54 to 49 pixels (5 actions).
The purple bar increased from 10 to 15 pixels (5 actions).
So the pattern is:
1. Initial state: 7 green blocks
2. After collecting all 7: blue regions merged, but level not complete
3. After moving agent RIGHT 5 times: 9 new green blocks appeared, blue regions split
This suggests the game has multiple phases where:
- Phase 1: Collect green blocks
- Phase 2: Move agent to trigger new green blocks
- Phase 3: Collect new green blocks
- And so on...
Let me collect the 9 green blocks now. I'll click on each one.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- Moving agent RIGHT triggered new green blocks to appear (9 total)
- 3 blocks in top area (rows 13-17), 6 blocks in 2x3 grid (rows 31-41)
- Blue region split into 2 separate regions again
- Pattern: collect blocks → move agent → new blocks appear → repeat
- Need to collect all 9 green blocks
Plan: Click on all 9 green blocks to collect them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Collect all 9 green blocks by clicking their centers
green_centers = [
    (15, 27), (15, 33), (15, 39),  # top row
    (33, 15), (33, 21), (33, 27),  # middle row 1
    (39, 15), (39, 21), (39, 27)   # middle row 2
]

# Click all 9 blocks
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in green_centers]
result = action(actions)
print(f"Result: {result}")

# Check if level completed
print(f"Level completed: {result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {result.get('done', False)}")

# Check remaining green blocks
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Remaining green blocks: {len(green_nodes)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
